title: Level 405
wikitext:
{{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 405: "Perennial Galleria"'''}} {{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Serif'; } }}{{Blurbackground|file=Abandoned skylight mall.jpg|}} {{Blurquote|image=121 Outlet.png||quote-text="Shopping malls across the country are dying fast, and my images of them are very nostalgic for most people that grew up attending these malls. These malls were communal spaces. These were gigantic chat rooms before the Internet existed. You went to the mall to meet and communicate with others, not just to shop."|person=Seph Lawless.}} {{Class Custom Image|class=Variable|hexagon= [[File:Level 121 "GOING UP".png|frameless|link=|style=display:block; margin-left:25]]|bg-image=Perennial Galleria.png|safety=Varying Regional Safety|stability=Variable Stability|entity=Exotic Entity Diversity|text-color= #4f2d00}}'''Level 405''' is the 406th level of The Backrooms.
| per·en·ni·al ( /pəˈrenēəl/ ) : lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time; enduring or continually recurring. | [ Please play this audio file for a more entertaining reading experience! ] {{Audio|align=middle|file=121 Ambience.ogg|volume=55|=true|loop=true}}
{{Borderimage|image=Perennial Galleria.png|align=center|width=500px|caption=Level 405's notorious exterior photo. Original photographer is unknown.|id=1}} =='''Description'''== '''Level 405''', also known as the '''''"Perennial Galleria"''''', is a dual-subsection level featuring both indoor and outdoor sections, housed within a large, well disguised industrial complex.
{{Borderimage|image=121 Outlet.png|align=right|width=350px|caption=A photo found on the ground inside '''Level 405''''s Outlet Mall.}|id=2}} ==='''Outdoors'''=== '''Level 405''''s outdoor facade is a large, entangled outlet mall, filled with labelless storefronts and empty walkways. This main entrance area of '''Level 405''' is a large, finite concrete room, roughly 30 feet tall. This room's walls fully surround the outlet area, giving the illusion of a horizon and sky. The concrete walls and ceiling surrounding the exterior outlet facade are hand painted, with small bits of paint stripped off over time. Lighting on the outside of '''Level 405''' stays at a constant golden hour, showering the front of the buildings in a deep amber glow.
Distorted city daytime ambience is heard playing on loop 24/7, blended with a looping playlist of 1990s-inspired Vaporwave and Mallsoft tracks, reminiscent of the music often heard in [[The Frontrooms|the Frontrooms]]. Mixed in with these are the sound of distant air-conditioning units and piping, leaving an ambient brown noise echoing throughout the outdoor facade area of '''Level 405'''. The false outdoor area reeks of bleach and air freshener, and all plant life is entirely fake, being made of rubbers and plastics. Metal lawn chairs and tables with exotically decorated umbrellas line the outdoor facade along the entrance to the indoor area. The entrance to the inside section of '''Level 405''' is marked by a large green archway overtop a large carousel door.
{{Borderimage|image=Topside of 121.jpg|align=center|width=450px|caption=A picture taken within the galleria interior.|id=3}} ==='''Indoors'''=== The interior of '''Level 405''' can be entered from the ''centermost entrance'' of the outdoor strip mall. The inside of '''Level 405''' is a large interior Galleria, designed similarly to those in the early 1990s, with structures and furnishings resembling that of the late 2000s and early 2010s. Inside, the building smells of chlorine and cleaning supplies, the sound of fountains creating a caustic white noise across the entire interior. Crusty overhead speakers play stereotypical mall music, and announcements made by a disembodied voice are heard every so often, discussing deals and advertisements for otherworldly products. {{Borderimage|image=The escalator.jpg|align=center|width=400px|caption=A picture of one of the aforementioned escalators.|id=6}}
The inside of '''Level 405''' spans into different wings, each wing being roughly 2 miles in length and 50 ft. wide from wall to wall. Stores displaying miscellaneous products sit on each side, with connecting bridges partitioned 30 feet apart on the balcony, lined occasionally with fully-functional escalators. ===='''Areas connected to these wings are:'''==== * A food court. * Large retail stores. * False exits leading to nowhere. The lights within the indoor section of '''Level 405''' shut off every 12 hours, acting as a day/night cycle. Items within the building get restocked daily during these cycles by '''''Staff''''' entities, leading to fresh new products entering shelves every 12 hour cycle. This also applies to food, as during these cycles, dishes are served in the food court fresh daily.
{{Borderimage|image=The Food Court.jpg|align=center|width=400px|caption='''Level 405’'''s infamous food court.|id=7}} =='''Lower Levels'''== The deeper sections of '''Level 405''', accessible by going down the escalators inside any of the large clothing stores at the end of the interior wings, lead farther down into the structure for an unknown amount of distance.
Going down floor by floor is a gamble, as these levels are considered sub-levels of '''Level 405''' itself. The deeper you go, the more sub-levels you enter. Each of these levels is intertwined with Level 405, and the entities and properties of each is untethered from each other, making each floor unique and completely different from the last.
{{Borderimage|image=405 where.jpg|align=center|width=400px|caption=Supposedly taken by [[Entity 1]] within an unidentified sub-level of '''Level 405'''.|id=8}}
=='''Topside Entities'''==
==='''Level 405''' has an extremely diverse and exotic entity list, here are just a few of the expected entities you'll encounter:=== ==='''''Staff'''''=== These humanoid entities range from tall to short, and lack most facial features besides eyes and hair. Staff entities resemble [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] humans, but their skin is described to be a thin layer of paper mache, covered with miscellaneous blots and streaks of paint, and seemingly lathered and built up with layers of art supplies. These entities adorn a painted on employee uniform, on which is a metal pin saying "Galleria Staff Member" in crayon. These entities are completely harmless, and act as guardians of the interior top floors of '''Level 405'''. ===== Note: These entities also regularly have small talk with wanderers! ===== ==='''''Steven'''''=== One very notable entity on '''Level 405''', locally known as '''''Steven''''', is a short, roughly 3 foot tall cardboard cutout, resembling the Staff entities. '''''Steven''''' is known to pop up in unexpected places, scaring unexpecting wanderers. He is entirely docile, and poses no threat to anyone. He only likes to scare people. '''''Steven''''''s aversion to cameras has prevented wanderers from capturing a clear photo of him. {{Borderimage|image=Steven.png|align=center|width=400px|caption=A rough portrait of "Steven" drawn on a used napkin.|id=4}} =='''Lower Level Entities'''== The deeper one goes into '''Level 405''', the more prevalent entities become, good and bad. These encounters are exotic and not much is known about the entities that reside in the sub-levels of '''Level 405'''. Exploration into these levels are currently under way. Expect detailed reports of sub-levels soon.
{{Collapse|num=2|initial= >> Sub-Level Expeditions << |final= << Collapse >> |body=!! EXPLORATIONS UNDERWAY. PLEASE WAIT FOR FURTHER INFORMATION !!
}} {{Borderimage|image=405 waterpark.webp|align=center|width=400px|caption=A photo taken by Staff De Galleria member 'Andrew Walker' 2 days before his mysterious disappearance.|id=5}} =='''Colonies and Outposts'''== '''''Staff De Galleria:''''' A group of wanderers who remain sheltered on the topmost floor of '''Level 405''''s interior building. These people have set up homes within the uppermost stores of the interior complex, using products and other materials found inside to their leisure. This group consists of an ever growing population of wanderers. '''''P.P.F. (Project Pillow Fort):''''' A group originating from '''[[Level 906.1]]'''. This group of veteran explorers have had access to '''Level 405''' via '''[[Level 524]]''''s escalator entry to the level. Since '''Staff De Galleria''' welcomes all traveling colonies, '''P.P.F.''' set up their own micro settlement inside '''Level 405''' for ease of travel. ------ =='''Entrances'''== ===''Due to recent events outside of colony control, some entrances and exits to '''Level 405''' have been destroyed.''===
*Ascending an escalator on '''[[Level 524]]''' with a sign reading "Going Up..." on it leads to the interior of '''Level 405'''. *Walking through any "Staff Only" door in the food court of [[The Delta Mall]] can lead to the food court of '''Level 405'''. *No-Clipping through a misplaced wall on [[Level 69]] leads to '''Level 405'''. *Entering a door labeled "405" on '''[[Level 21]]''' leads to '''Level 405'''. *Following the scent of air-freshener and chemical bleach through a tight vent on [[Level 194]] leads to '''Level 405'''. =='''Exits'''== *Descending an escalator on '''Level 405''' with a sign reading "Going Down..." on it leads to [[Level 524|'''Level 524''']]. *Going deep into '''Level 405''' can possibly lead to '''''anywhere''''' in [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. *Exiting a "Staff Only" door in the food court can possibly backtrack to [[The Delta Mall]]. *Jumping into a fountain with a translucent white bottom noclips one to [[Level 37]]. *Exiting through a tight vent in the exterior facade walls lead to [[Level 194]]. *Noclipping through a fountain in sends one to [[Level 0.8]].
